description:
This quiz assesses knowledge of HTML5 semantic elements beyond basic tags like <div> and <span>, gauging both conceptual understanding and practical usage. It is positioned as a moderate‑difficulty test with a light‑hearted, encouraging tone that nudges learners to adopt better markup practices. The style mixes humor with a challenge, aiming to motivate developers to improve their HTML skills.
Topics: HTML5 semantic tags (header, nav, article, section, footer, etc.), Proper use of structural elements, Accessibility considerations in markup, Differences between generic and semantic elements
Audience: Front‑end developers, web designers, and anyone with basic HTML experience who wants to validate and deepen their understanding of modern, semantic HTML.
